do_not_merge.reason: Contradiction test: different entity_type (agency vs transit_authority) for the same real-world MTA entity. Canon forces do_not_merge despite shared identity keys.
Contradiction test: different entity_type (agency vs public benefit corporation).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (transit_authority vs public benefit corporation).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (transit_authority vs agency).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (department vs agency). Department of Buses is a sub-unit of NYCT, not the same canonical identity.
Contradiction test: different entity_type (department vs agency).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (public benefit corporation subsidiary vs committee). LIRR and its oversight committee are distinct.
Contradiction test: different entity_type (committee vs transit_authority).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (committee vs public benefit corporation subsidiary).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (committee vs agency).
Contradiction test: different entity_type (agency vs transit_authority). FMTAC is a captive insurer, not the MTA itself.
Contradiction test: person vs agency. A person cannot merge with an agency.
Contradiction test: different entity_type (person vs government_official). Both represent Frank Farrell but the entity_type differs; canon treats these as distinct strong fields.
Contradiction test: person vs transit_authority.
Shared identity key entity_lisa-sorin is erroneous; these are different persons (Lisa Sorin vs Catherine Sheridan). Entity_name differs strongly.
Different legal entities: MaBSTOA and MTA Bus Company are separate operating authorities. The shared identity key is a collision, not a merge.
Different entity_type: board committee vs agency. The committee oversees Metro-North but is not the railroad itself.
Different entity_type: agency vs agency (NYCT vs MTA Headquarters). Also different identity: NYCT is a subsidiary, MTA HQ is a support unit.
Different entity_type: agency vs paratransit service program. Access-a-Ride is a program under NYCT, not the same entity.
Different entity_type: agency vs person.
Both are committees, but entity_nyct-mta-bus-committee-charter is the Committee on Operations of NYCT/etc., which is a different committee from the NYC Transit Committee.
Contradiction test: different entity_type (office vs agency department). Despite shared name, the types differ.
Contradiction test: entity_type agency vs department. The real estate department is a sub-unit of MTA, not an agency.
Contradiction test: entity_type department vs agency department. Both represent the same department but types differ; cannot merge.
Different entity_type: division vs transit_authority. Risk management is a division of MTA, not the MTA itself.
Different entity_type: agency vs person.
Different entity_type: board vs transit_authority. The board governs the MTA but is a distinct entity.
Different entity_type: governing body vs transit_authority.
Different entity_type: office vs transit_authority.
Different entity_type: paratransit service program vs transit_authority.

weak_aliases.reason: Acronym and legal name merge per canon, but entity_type differences with other MTA records prevent merge. These aliases are valid for search.

needed_context: Verify if entity_type 'person' vs 'government_official' is a meaningful distinction in the model; if both are treated as natural persons, they should merge.
Check whether entity_type 'agency', 'department', 'agency department' are interchangeable for MTA subunits; if so, merge.
Determine if 'office' and 'agency department' are considered the same strong identity field for MTA internal units.

suggested_rules: Consider a rule: for MTA parent entity, entity_type variations (agency/transit_authority/public benefit corporation) should be treated as aliases of the same strong identity, since they all refer to the same legal entity.
Consider a rule: for MTA internal departments/offices, entity_type differences like department vs agency department vs office should not force do_not_merge if they share the same name and parent.
suggestion.rationale: Applied Identity Merge Canon strictly. Merge groups consist of records with the same entity_type and shared identity keys. Do_not_merge pairs are primarily due to entity_type contradictions (test 1). Ambiguous cases involve entity_type granularity for MTA subunits; evidence from payload suggests the same real-world objects but different entity_type labels. Suggested rules could refine future handling.
